content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 1,
1995
Kathleen A.
Blomo
Assistant to the City Manager
P.O.
Box 667
Chino,
CA
91708—0667
RE:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
1—95—129
Dear Ms.
Blomo:
This letter is in response to your request for advice under
the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
/ Because
your request seeks general guidance specific to no particular
individual,
we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance. 2/
OUESTIONS
1.
How would the use of a parking pass issued to the City.
of Chino by the Ontario Airport be reported by city officials when
used in the course of city business?
2.
How would use of the same pass be reported by city
officials when used for personal purposes?
CONCLUSION
The use of a parking pass by a city official, whether in the
course of city business or for personal purposes,
is not
considered a gift to the official if the pass was provided in
conformance with the criteria in Regulation 18944.1
(copy
enclosed).
Under such circumstances the city official is not
required to report the use of the parking permit on his/her
statement of economic interests.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the reguestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1—95—129
Page
2
FACTS
The City of Chino has been given an unconditional use parking
pass by the Ontario Airport management.
The pass is used by
councilmembers and city staff.
While speaking with you to obtain
additional information, you indicated that the pass may or may not
be used for personal purposes.
ANALYSIS
Your question is, if required,
how would use of a parking
pass issued to the City of Chino by the Ontario Airport be
reported by a city official when used in the course of city
business and/or when used for personal purposes.
Regulation 18944.1 states,
in part:
Passes or tickets which provide admission or access to
facilities...which are provided to an agency official
are not gifts to the official whenever...
(a)
The donor gives the tickets or passes to the
official’s agency, through a responsible official
of the agency, for the sole purpose of distributing
the passes or tickets to officials of the agency
and their spouses and immediate families and use of
the tickets or passes is so limited by the agency;
and
The tickets or passes are not earmarked by the
donor for any specific agency officials; and
The agency retains a written public record of the
terms under which the tickets were accepted by the
agency and the terms under which the tickets or
passes were distributed and to whom they were
distributed....
Therefore, if a parking pass is given to the city according
to the criteria set out in Regulation 18944.1,
no reporting
obligations are incurred by city officials who use the pass for
business or personal purposes.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested.
If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
J’2%441 )Lbv
Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
